subject: Update TOSCA PoC with changes from demo integration
commit_message:
Update TOSCA PoC with changes from demo integration

This commit includes demo version changes for establishing a PMSH controlloop.
Apologies for bringing a huge commit, this code is for the
ControlLoop Demo, and it can act as base for the actual release.
This framework is going to be completely changed including Spring
framework for the future release. Please consider this code as base for
any future work on controlloop.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3215
Signed-off-by: Sirisha_Manchikanti <sirisha.manchikanti@est.tech>
Change-Id: I512626295d95ab32c29c6861375d31228d549ce9

Patch Set 1:

(82 comments)

it appears that a number of changes made in response to comments that were made in previous reviews have been lost.

also, noticed a number of files with hard-coded ports and DB names that used to be ${port} and ${dbName}.  Do those matter?  If these files are used in junit tests, then I would think we would want to avoid hard-coded ports.

Patch Set 1:

ONAp generally frowns on anything >10K lines. As a side note, why spring? Spring to me is a lot of luggage and they typically have security issues that require constant upgrade. What is the benefit to moving to that? Perhaps we can take that question up in a Wednesday meeting.

> 
> ONAp generally frowns on anything >10K lines. As a side note, why spring? Spring to me is a lot of luggage and they typically have security issues that require constant upgrade. What is the benefit to moving to that? Perhaps we can take that question up in a Wednesday meeting.

Spring was suggested by Seb in the CLAMP team, se the discussion here:
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/TOSCA+based+Control+Loops+with+the+existing+CLAMP+code+in+the+Policy+Framework

On the review size, as we were integrating the PoC demo, we discovered a number of small fixes that needed to be made in a number of different parts of the code so it is difficult to break the review up into smaller chunks. Also, the large size is due to test files and configuration files much more than code.

This PoC code is in its own branch now. We will not merge this code into master directly but instead transfer parts of it across to the Master branch in smaller chunks, while more of it will be integrated with the existing CLAMP code.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-clamp-tosca-poc-verify-java/202/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-clamp-tosca-poc-verify-java/202

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > ONAp generally frowns on anything >10K lines. As a side note, why spring? Spring to me is a lot of luggage and they typically have security issues that require constant upgrade. What is the benefit to moving to that? Perhaps we can take that question up in a Wednesday meeting.
> 
> Spring was suggested by Seb in the CLAMP team, se the discussion here:
> https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/TOSCA+based+Control+Loops+with+the+existing+CLAMP+code+in+the+Policy+Framework

Well, 
When we got Clamp a while ago, we had more than 150K lines for the UI & the Backend. Thanks to SpringBoot & React + generic code introduced we have reduced it to 15K(+2or3K in Camel) lines for everything. So it helped 😄

As an example: Initially we had SQL stored procedure to manage the Crud operations and the transactions were done by hand (+ all bugs potentially related to that, bad locking strategy, etc ...). By using SpringBoot JTA/JPA/Beans + hibernate JSONable POJO, we were able to reduce significantly the amount of code to manage the DB to nearly nothing. And everything is out of the box, no need to have complex configuration (add dep in pom.xml and you are good to go).

Liam, I think the Spring analysis is currently done by your team right ? 
So the questions that need to be answered now are: For the policy code if you use SpringBoot/hibernate&JTA/SpringMVC "does it reduce the amount of code, can you kick some stuffs?", "does it simplify your day to day work", "Does it improve the maintainability ?"

By using SpringBoot JTA/JPA/Beans + hibernate JSONable POJO, we were able to reduce significantly the amount of code to manage the DB to nearly nothing. And everything is out of the box, no need to have complex configuration (add dep in pom.xml and you are good to go).
>

It would be good to take this discussion at a Policy Framework meeting, perhaps next Wednesday.

We are using this review as an "archiving"review, once this review is in the toca-poc branch, we have one more review to bring in and archive the angular GUI we built for the PoC. So the tosca-poc branch will then have exactly the code we used in the PoC.
